raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2025 I placed an on line order for {$77.00} with a company that claimed to be a USA company. My credit card was instantly over charged for {$88.00} by a merchant in XXXX XXXX, so I immediately requested the order be cancelled and alerted my credit card company, Citi Bank. Citi Bank instructed me to let the charge post to my account, then dispute it, which I did. I discovered that the merchant was highly known for fraudulent activity and provided several documents of proof of this to Citi Bank. I received several emails days later from the merchant stating I could not cancel my order and they provided me with a fake tracking number. Later they claimed my order was delivered and I received nothing from them. 
Citi Bank has been " investigating '' my dispute for over two months and are now telling me that because I authorized the charge, it is not considered fraud. Regardless, I still have not received anything from this merchant who is well known for scamming people. I just want Citi Bank to resolve this matter and issue me a permanent credit.
